programs.name: Elderly and Persons with Physical Disabilities (EPD) Waiver Program
programs.tagline: If your loved one needs daily help with bathing or dressing but wants to stay home, this DC program covers personal care aides and adult day services.
programs.intro:
If your loved one is 65+ or has a physical disability and needs daily help with bathing, dressing, or medication management, DC's EPD Waiver covers personal care aides, adult day health services, and respite care so they can stay in their home or assisted living instead of a nursing facility.

The program requires nursing facility level of care (determined through a clinical assessment) but allows your loved one to receive those services at home. Services include case management, homemaker services, personal care aides, respite care, and environmental modifications. Room and board are not covered.

Income limit is $2,743/month for an individual (2023), with assets limited to $4,000. The program has a waitlist on a first-come, first-served basis, and you must be Medicaid-eligible or meet Medicaid spend-down requirements.

summary: Need nursing facility level of care
structuredEligibility.functionalRequirement: Must require nursing facility level of care, determined by face-to-face assessment showing need for assistance with at least 2 activities of daily living (eating, dressing, toileting, bathing)
otherRequirements: US citizen or qualified immigration status for Medicaid

applicationGuide.summary: Contact DACL Medicaid Services Enrollment Unit for assistance, they coordinate the complete application including required assessments, with eligibility determination within 45 days.

steps.title: Contact DACL for application assistance
steps.description: Call DACL Medicaid Services Enrollment Unit (MES) for help with coordination and submission. They will link you to case management and guide you through the process.

steps.title: Get physician assessment
steps.description: Have your loved one's physician or advanced practice registered nurse complete and sign the required Prescription Order Form (POF). This medical form is mandatory for application.

steps.title: Complete Liberty Healthcare assessment
steps.description: Schedule a face-to-face assessment with Liberty Healthcare to determine nursing facility level of care. This clinical assessment evaluates your loved one's need for daily assistance.

steps.title: Submit complete application
steps.description: The Aging and Disability Resource Center (ADRC) submits your complete application to Economic Security Administration (ESA) once all documents and assessments are ready.

steps.title: Wait for eligibility decision
steps.description: ESA determines eligibility within 45 calendar days after receiving your complete application. If approved, you'll be placed on the waitlist for services.

applicationGuide.processingTime: 45 calendar days after complete application is submitted
applicationGuide.waitlist: Program has a waiting list on first-come, first-served basis
applicationGuide.tip: You can apply without already having Medicaid, but you must be Medicaid-eligible or meet spend-down requirements to qualify.

documentsNeeded: Valid government-issued photo ID
Proof of DC residency (utility bill, lease agreement, or state-issued document)
Proof of US citizenship or qualified immigration status
Social Security card
Most recent Social Security award letter
Pension or retirement income statements
Bank statements for all accounts (last 3 months)
Investment documents (stocks, bonds, annuities)
Documentation showing countable assets under $4,000
Completed Prescription Order Form (POF) signed by physician or advanced practice registered nurse
Liberty Healthcare face-to-face assessment results
Medicare card (if applicable)
Current health insurance information
List of current medications and medical conditions
Contact information for current healthcare providers

contacts.label: DACL Medicaid Services Enrollment Unit
contacts.description: Primary contact for EPD Waiver application assistance and coordination

contacts.label: Liberty Healthcare
contacts.description: Required face-to-face assessments for nursing facility level of care determination

contacts.label: DC 311
contacts.description: General DC government services and referrals

applicationNotes: Application is incomplete without both the physician-signed Prescription Order Form and Liberty Healthcare assessment, gather these first
Income limit is 300% of SSI and updates annually, verify current year limits before applying
Asset limit is strictly $4,000 for individuals with no household size variations, plan spend-down if over this amount
Being on the waitlist doesn't guarantee services, it's first-come, first-served basis and wait times vary

faqs.question: Can my loved one apply if they're not already on Medicaid?
faqs.answer: Yes, your loved one can apply without already having Medicaid, but they must be Medicaid-eligible or meet Medicaid spend-down requirements to qualify for the EPD Waiver. The program will help determine Medicaid eligibility as part of the process.

faqs.question: How long is the waitlist really?
faqs.answer: The program operates on a first-come, first-served basis, but specific wait times aren't published. Contact DACL Medicaid Services Enrollment Unit for current waitlist information, as this can vary significantly based on funding and capacity.

faqs.question: What if my loved one's assets are slightly over $4,000?
faqs.answer: The $4,000 asset limit is strict for individuals. Your loved one would need to spend down assets below this limit before qualifying. Consider paying for medical expenses, home modifications, or other allowable expenses to meet the requirement.

faqs.question: Can I apply on behalf of my elderly loved one?
faqs.answer: Yes, family members can help with the application process, but your loved one will still need to participate in the required face-to-face assessment with Liberty Healthcare. Contact DACL Medicaid Services Enrollment Unit to discuss your specific situation.

faqs.question: What's the difference between home care and assisted living coverage?
faqs.answer: The EPD Waiver covers services in both your loved one's home and in assisted living facilities. However, room and board costs in assisted living are not covered, only the care services themselves.

faqs.question: What happens if my loved one's condition changes after enrollment?
faqs.answer: Your loved one will have ongoing case management to monitor their needs. If their condition improves significantly and they no longer need nursing facility level of care, they may lose eligibility. If needs increase, services can be adjusted within the program.

faqs.question: Can my loved one have both Medicare and EPD Waiver services?
faqs.answer: Yes, EPD Waiver services complement Medicare and Medicaid coverage. The waiver covers long-term care services that Medicare typically doesn't provide, such as personal care aides and adult day health services.

faqs.question: What if the Liberty Healthcare assessment doesn't find nursing facility level of care?
faqs.answer: If the assessment doesn't establish nursing facility level of care (requiring assistance with at least 2 activities of daily living), your loved one won't qualify for the EPD Waiver. You can appeal this decision or explore other DC programs for lower levels of care needs.

programs.name: Safe at Home Program
programs.tagline: Your loved one can get up to $7,000 in home safety modifications like grab bars, stair lifts, and bathtub cuts to prevent falls.

localEntities.name: Administered by Department of Aging and Community Living (DACL)
Historically administered through Home Care Partners (DCOA grantee) in partnership with District of Columbia Office on Aging and Department of Housing and Community Development

programs.intro:
If your loved one is 60+ or has a disability and lives in DC, they may qualify for up to $7,000 in home modifications to prevent falls. The program covers grab bars, handrails, stair lifts, bathtub cuts, shower seats, and even security cameras, modifications that can help your loved one stay safely in their home instead of moving to a facility.

Because this program focuses specifically on fall prevention, your loved one will need to pass a falls risk assessment to determine their maximum benefit amount. Income limits are $72,550/year for a single person or $82,550/year for a married couple, though higher-income households may qualify with a cost-share component.

The catch: 60% of applications are rejected for incomplete documentation, and your loved one can only use this program twice in their lifetime. If their needs exceed $7,000 or they're considered high fall risk, they'll be automatically referred to a different DC housing program.
programs.savingsSource: Maximum grant amount per residence from DC Department of Aging and Community Living

summary: Must own home or have owner permission
structuredEligibility.functionalRequirement: Must pass falls risk and fear of falling assessment conducted by the program to determine benefit level
otherRequirements: Property must be primary residence (live there 50%+ of year)
Homeowner or renter with written owner permission for modifications

applicationGuide.summary: Call (202) 724-5626 to request an application, processing time not specified but thorough documentation is critical.

steps.title: Call DACL
steps.description: Call (202) 724-5626 to request a Safe at Home grant application form and schedule your falls risk assessment.

steps.title: Gather all documentation
steps.description: Collect proof of DC residency, income, property ownership (or written landlord permission), and disability documentation if under 60. Missing documents cause 60% of rejections.

steps.title: Complete falls risk assessment
steps.description: DACL will conduct a falls risk and fear of falling assessment to determine your maximum benefit amount (up to $7,000 for low-risk clients).

steps.title: Submit complete application
steps.description: Mail or deliver your completed application with all required documentation to the Department of Aging and Community Living.

steps.title: Await approval and installation
steps.description: If approved, contractors will install approved modifications. Projects exceeding $7,000 or high-risk clients are referred to DHCD's rehabilitation program.

applicationGuide.tip: Prepare all documentation before calling, incomplete applications are the top reason for rejection

documentsNeeded: Valid government-issued photo ID
Proof of DC residency (utility bill, lease, or voter registration)
Proof that property is your primary residence
Property deed or mortgage documents (homeowners)
Written permission from property owner to make modifications (renters)
Proof of disability documentation (waived if 60+ years old)
Proof of household income from all sources
Social Security award letters for all household members
Bank statements for all accounts (last 3 months)
Most recent tax return or tax transcript
Pension or retirement income statements
Any other income documentation (unemployment, disability, etc.)

contacts.label: DC 311
contacts.description: General DC government services and transfers

applicationNotes: You can only use this program twice in your lifetime, plan your modifications carefully
If your needs exceed $7,000 or you're deemed high fall risk, you'll be automatically referred to DHCD's Single Family Residential Rehabilitation Program
Renters must get written permission from their landlord before applying, this is required documentation
The program has two income tiers: base eligibility at 80% AMI and expanded eligibility at 100% AMI with cost-sharing, confirm current limits when applying
relatedPrograms: DHCD Single Family Residential Rehabilitation Program

faqs.question: Can my loved one apply if they rent their home?
faqs.answer: Yes, but they must get written permission from their landlord to make the modifications. This written permission must be submitted with the application. Without landlord approval, the application will be rejected.

faqs.question: What happens if my loved one needs more than $7,000 in modifications?
faqs.answer: They'll be automatically referred to DC's Department of Housing and Community Development Single Family Residential Rehabilitation Program, which handles larger projects. The Safe at Home Program cannot exceed its $7,000 limit.

faqs.question: How many times can my loved one use this program?
faqs.answer: Only twice in their lifetime. Each use is called an 'episode' and covers a separate set of modifications. Plan carefully since there's no third opportunity.

faqs.question: What if my loved one's income is slightly above $72,550?
faqs.answer: They may still qualify under the cost-share component with income up to $99,600 for individuals or $113,850 for couples. Call (202) 724-5626 to confirm current thresholds and cost-share requirements.

faqs.question: Can I apply on behalf of my elderly loved one?
faqs.answer: Yes, family members can help with the application process. However, your loved one will need to be present for the required falls risk assessment since it evaluates their specific mobility and safety needs.

faqs.question: What if my loved one fails the falls risk assessment?
faqs.answer: There's no 'failing', the assessment determines benefit level. High fall risk clients are referred to DHCD's larger rehabilitation program for more comprehensive modifications, while lower risk clients receive up to $7,000 through Safe at Home.

faqs.question: Are these grants taxable income?
faqs.answer: No, Safe at Home grants are specifically exempt from District of Columbia income taxation. Your loved one won't owe DC taxes on the modification value.

faqs.question: Why do so many applications get rejected?
faqs.answer: 60% of applications are rejected for incomplete documentation. The most common missing items are proof of residency, income verification, and landlord permission for renters. Gather everything before applying.

programs.name: DACL Benefits Assistance
programs.tagline: Get help enrolling your DC loved one in Medicaid home care programs that could cover personal care aides and case management.
programs.intro:
If your loved one is 65+ or has a disability and needs help with daily tasks like bathing, dressing, or medication management, DACL Benefits Assistance helps them enroll in DC's Elderly and Persons with Physical Disabilities (EPD) Waiver program. This Medicaid waiver covers personal care aide services, case management, and other supports that let your loved one stay home instead of moving to a nursing home.

Your loved one must qualify for Medicaid with income under $2,743/month and countable assets under $4,000 (2023 figures). Because this program requires nursing home level of care, your loved one will need a face-to-face assessment by Liberty Healthcare to determine their level of need. DACL staff guide families through the entire enrollment process, from Medicaid applications to waiver services.

This is enrollment assistance, not direct benefits, DACL helps you navigate the complex EPD Waiver system and connects you with the right case managers once approved.

summary: Needs nursing home level of care

countedAssets: Cash value of life insurance over $10,000

structuredEligibility.functionalRequirement: Must need nursing home level of care (requires daily help with bathing, dressing, medication management, or other activities of daily living) as determined by Liberty Healthcare face-to-face assessment
otherRequirements: US citizen or qualified immigration status
DC Medicaid provider must complete Prescription Order Form

applicationGuide.summary: Call DACL at (202) 724-5626, they handle the entire process including Medicaid enrollment and waiver coordination, with eligibility determined within 45 days.

steps.title: Call DACL Medicaid Enrollment Services
steps.description: Contact (202) 724-5626 to speak with DACL staff who will assess your loved one's situation and guide you through the process.

steps.title: Schedule ADRC appointment if needed
steps.description: DACL may schedule an in-person appointment at the Aging and Disability Resource Center to review documents and complete initial screening.

steps.title: Get doctor to complete Prescription Order Form
steps.description: A DC Medicaid provider must complete the required Prescription Order Form (POF) documenting your loved one's medical need for services.

steps.title: Liberty Healthcare assessment
steps.description: Liberty Healthcare will schedule a face-to-face assessment at your loved one's home to evaluate their level of care needs.

steps.title: Submit complete application
steps.description: DACL helps coordinate submission of all required documents to the Economic Security Administration for final eligibility determination.

applicationGuide.processingTime: 45 calendar days after complete application submission
applicationGuide.tip: Have your loved one's DC Medicaid provider complete the Prescription Order Form early, this often causes delays if missing.

documentsNeeded: Valid government-issued photo ID
Proof of DC residency (utility bill, lease, or voter registration)
Birth certificate or passport for age verification
Social Security card
Most recent Social Security award letter
Medicare card (both parts A and B)
Bank statements for all accounts (last 3 months)
Investment account statements (stocks, bonds, annuities)
Life insurance policies showing cash value
Prescription Order Form (POF) completed by DC Medicaid provider
Medical records documenting need for daily assistance
Property deeds or mortgage statements
Vehicle titles and registration
Burial insurance or pre-need funeral contracts

contacts.label: DACL Medicaid Enrollment Services
contacts.description: Primary contact for DACL Benefits Assistance enrollment help

contacts.label: DC 211
contacts.description: 24/7 helpline for all DC social services and programs

applicationNotes: Income and asset limits in sources may be outdated (2021-2023 figures), verify current amounts as they're tied to 300% of SSI
The Prescription Order Form from a DC Medicaid provider is required and often causes application delays if not completed properly
This is primarily enrollment assistance for the EPD Waiver, you'll still need separate Medicaid eligibility and may need spend-down if income is over limit
Face-to-face assessment by Liberty Healthcare is mandatory and must show nursing home level of care need

faqs.question: Can my loved one keep their house if it's worth more than $4,000?
faqs.answer: Yes, your loved one's primary home doesn't count toward the $4,000 asset limit. The limit only applies to countable assets like bank accounts, investments, and cash value of life insurance over $10,000.

faqs.question: What if my loved one's income is over $2,743 per month?
faqs.answer: Your loved one may still qualify through Medicaid spend-down, where they pay their excess income toward medical expenses each month. DACL staff can explain how spend-down works and help calculate the amount.

faqs.question: How long does the Liberty Healthcare assessment take?
faqs.answer: The face-to-face assessment typically takes 1-2 hours and must be done in your loved one's home or assisted living facility. Liberty Healthcare will schedule this after the initial DACL screening.

faqs.question: Can I apply on behalf of my elderly loved one?
faqs.answer: Yes, adult children can help with the application process. If your loved one cannot sign documents due to cognitive issues, you may need power of attorney or guardianship documentation.

faqs.question: What happens if my loved one doesn't qualify for nursing home level of care?
faqs.answer: If the Liberty Healthcare assessment shows your loved one doesn't meet nursing home level of care, they won't qualify for EPD Waiver services. DACL may refer you to other community programs that require less intensive care needs.

faqs.question: Can my loved one get services while living in assisted living?
faqs.answer: Yes, EPD Waiver services can be provided in assisted living facilities, not just private homes. The assessment will determine what additional services are needed beyond what the facility provides.

faqs.question: What if we can't find a DC Medicaid provider to complete the Prescription Order Form?
faqs.answer: Contact DACL at (202) 724-5626 for help finding DC Medicaid providers in your area who can complete the required POF. This is a common barrier they help families navigate.

faqs.question: How is this different from applying for Medicaid directly?
faqs.answer: DACL provides specialized help navigating both Medicaid enrollment and the complex EPD Waiver system. They coordinate between multiple agencies (ESA, Liberty Healthcare, case management) so you don't have to manage each piece separately.

programs.name: DC Senior Centers
programs.tagline: Free social activities, meals, health screenings, and support services for DC residents 60 and older.

localEntities.name: SOME Senior Services (1395 Aspen St. NW)
Vida Senior Centers (multiple sites for Hispanic seniors)

programs.intro:
If your loved one is 60 or older and lives in DC, they can access free meals, health screenings, social activities, and case management support through the city's network of senior centers. These centers provide daily programming like exercise classes, nutritional counseling, blood pressure checks, and mental health support, often with no cost or sliding scale fees based on ability to pay.

DC operates multiple senior centers across the city, each with different specialties and hours. SOME Senior Services focuses on general programming but excludes residents with dementia diagnoses, while Vida Senior Centers specifically serves Hispanic seniors with Spanish-language programming. Most centers offer services Monday through Friday with varying schedules, and many provide free congregate meals as part of their daily programming.

summary: Able to complete daily activities independently
structuredEligibility.functionalRequirement: Must be able to complete daily living activities independently or with a personal care assistant. Some centers exclude participants with dementia or Alzheimer's diagnoses.
otherRequirements: US citizen or eligible immigration status for some services

applicationGuide.summary: Visit any senior center directly for immediate intake assessment, no formal application required.

steps.title: Choose a center
steps.description: Contact the D.C. Office on Aging at (202) 724-7000 for referrals to centers near your loved one, or visit centers directly like SOME at 1395 Aspen St. NW or Vida Senior Centers for Hispanic seniors.

steps.title: Visit for intake screening
steps.description: Bring proof of DC residency and age to the center. Staff will conduct an eligibility screening and explain available services during your visit.

steps.title: Start participating
steps.description: Most services begin immediately after intake. Centers will explain their specific schedules, meal times, and activity calendars.

applicationGuide.processingTime: Immediate intake at centers upon eligibility screening
applicationGuide.tip: Each center has different rules and specialties, call ahead to confirm they can serve your loved one's specific needs, especially if they have dementia or prefer services in Spanish.

documentsNeeded: Valid government-issued photo ID or birth certificate (proof of age)
Proof of DC residency (utility bill, lease, or DC-issued document)
Income verification if applying for sliding-scale fee programs
Medical records if requesting functional assessment for specialized services

contacts.label: D.C. Office on Aging
contacts.description: General information and referrals to senior centers

contacts.label: SOME Senior Services
contacts.description: Northwest DC senior center at 1395 Aspen St. NW

faqs.question: Can my loved one with dementia participate in DC senior centers?
faqs.answer: It depends on the specific center. SOME Senior Services excludes participants with dementia or Alzheimer's diagnoses, but other centers in the DC network may accept participants with cognitive impairments. Call the D.C. Office on Aging at (202) 724-7000 to find centers that can accommodate your loved one's needs.

faqs.question: What if my loved one doesn't speak English well?
faqs.answer: Vida Senior Centers specifically serve Hispanic seniors with programming conducted in Spanish. Contact the D.C. Office on Aging at (202) 724-7000 to locate Vida center locations or other centers that may offer services in your loved one's preferred language.

faqs.question: How much do the meals and activities cost?
faqs.answer: Most DC senior centers operate on voluntary contributions or sliding scale fees based on ability to pay. Many services including congregate meals are completely free for DC residents age 60+. Each center sets its own fee structure, so ask about costs during your intake visit.

stateOverview.intro: If your loved one lives in DC and needs daily help staying independent, the District offers 3 benefit programs that could cover thousands in home care services and safety modifications, plus free resources for all seniors 60+. The most valuable is DC's EPD Waiver Program, which covers personal care aides and adult day services for seniors who qualify for nursing home care but want to stay home. DC also provides up to $7,000 in home safety modifications through the Safe at Home Program and dedicated help navigating Medicaid home care programs through DACL Benefits Assistance. Start with the EPD Waiver if your loved one needs daily personal care, it offers the most comprehensive coverage.

startHere.name: Elderly and Persons with Physical Disabilities (EPD) Waiver Program
startHere.why: Covers personal care aides and adult day services if your loved one needs nursing home level care but wants to stay home.

startHere.name: Safe at Home Program
startHere.why: Provides up to $7,000 in home modifications like grab bars and stair lifts to prevent falls.

startHere.name: DACL Benefits Assistance
startHere.why: Free help enrolling your loved one in DC Medicaid home care programs that cover personal care and case management.

programs: Elderly and Persons with Physical Disabilities (EPD) Waiver Program
byNeed.description: Covers personal care aides and adult day services for seniors who qualify for nursing home care.
Up to $7,000 for grab bars, stair lifts, and bathtub cuts to prevent falls.
Free assistance enrolling in DC Medicaid home care programs.
Free services for all DC residents 60+ including meals, health screenings, and social programs.

quickFacts: DC has 3 benefit programs that require qualification based on income, disability, or care needs
The EPD Waiver Program is DC's main alternative to nursing home placement for seniors who need daily care
All DC residents 60+ can access free senior center services regardless of income
stateOverview.resourcesVsBenefits: DC offers both qualification-based benefits and free resources. Benefits like the EPD Waiver Program and Safe at Home Program require meeting specific income or disability criteria and provide significant financial assistance for home care and modifications. Free resources like DC Senior Centers are available to all residents 60+ regardless of income and provide social activities, meals, and health screenings at no cost.
